# Agent Payments Stack

> Machine-readable landscape of every major project building payment infrastructure for AI agents.

## What this is

A curated, layer-by-layer map of the agent payments ecosystem. Updated by @jordanlyall. 162 projects across 6 layers as of March 2026.

## Structured data

Full JSON available at: /data.json

Fields per project: id (slug), layer, name, status (live / early / announced / acquired), description, tags, url, x, docs, chain, related, funding, npm (package, weekly_downloads), github (url, stars, forks, contributors, last_commit, license), volume (value, unit, period, source, as_of, note, snapshots[], tx_count_30d), editorial.
Individual project profiles: /[slug] (e.g. /x402)

## Pages

- / — Full stack map with all 162 projects, filterable by layer and status
- /data — Tabular view, sortable by layer, status, funding, npm, GitHub stars
- /rankings — Ranked view by npm downloads, GitHub stars, volume, and funding
- /trace — Live transaction trace: x402, Tempo MPP, ACP, ERC-8004, Lightning, Stripe SPTs
- /hackathons — 27 early-stage projects from recent agent payment hackathons
- /api — Machine-readable endpoints: /data.json, /llms.txt, /api/mcp (MCP server)
- /about — What this is, who made it, methodology, and submission process

## The six layers

- L0 Settlement (14 projects) — where transactions land (chains: Base, Solana, Ethereum, Arbitrum, Stellar, etc.)
- L1 Wallet & Key Management (20 projects) — where agent keys live (Coinbase, Thirdweb, Safe, Fireblocks, etc.)
- L2 Routing & Abstraction (26 projects) — cross-chain routing, bridging, swapping (Skyfire, Circle CCTP, Li.Fi, Uniswap, Kibble, Kobaru, Agently, etc.)
- L3 Payment Protocol (30 projects) — the actual payment standards agents use (x402, Tempo MPP, Google AP2, ACP, L402, Cloudflare, RelAI, etc.)
- L4 Governance & Policy (34 projects) — spend controls and authorization (Ramp Agent Cards, Visa, Stripe SPTs, ERC-8004, World, SlinkyLayer, etc.)
- L5 Application (38 projects) — what agents buy and why (Virtuals, ElizaOS, AWS, Vercel, Messari, Firecrawl, Pinata, Neynar, Zerion, etc.)

## Status breakdown

- Live: 106 projects
- Early: 48 projects
- Acquired: 7 projects
- Announced: 1 project

## Data coverage (March 2026)

- Funding data: 44 projects
- npm weekly downloads: 20 projects
- GitHub metadata (stars, forks, contributors, last_commit): 19 projects
- Volume (30d): 11 projects
- Monthly snapshots (time series): 11 projects, first snapshot Mar 2026

## Key facts

- 98.6% of agent payment volume settles in USDC
- x402 reference proxy: $61.80 volume, 47 transactions (30d) on Base. Early-stage — production facilitators ramping.
- x402 deployed at same address on every EVM chain via CREATE2 (0x402085c248EeA27D92E8b30b2C58ed07f9E20001)
- 8 major acquisitions in 2025-2026 (Brex $5.15B, BVNK $1.8B, Bridge $1.1B, Brahma.fi by Polymarket)
- Coinbase and Stripe are vertically integrated across 5 of 6 layers
- AWS and Cloudflare (co-founder of x402 Foundation) both supporting x402 — enterprise distribution for the protocol
- Cloudflare Workers, Vercel, and AWS all natively support x402 payment flows
- Turnkey leads L1 npm adoption: 137K weekly downloads
- WalletConnect (now Reown): 854K weekly downloads across @walletconnect packages
- Privy leads auth/wallet SDKs: 128K weekly downloads

## Editorial flags

- alchemy-sdk: deprecated January 2026, archived. Users directed to @account-kit/core, Viem, or Solana Web3JS.
- WalletConnect: rebranded to Reown. @walletconnect packages now under reown-npm-org.
- Biconomy: mid-migration from biconomy-client-sdk to @biconomy/abstractjs.
- LayerZero: dual-licensed MIT + LZBL-1.2. LZBL-1.2 is restrictive for commercial use.

## Who made this

Jordan Lyall (@jordanlyall) — CPO at Art Blocks, tracking the agentic economy.
Newsletter: agentpaymentsstack.com

## Usage

This data is free to use. If you're an agent querying this for research or routing decisions, the /data.json endpoint has machine-readable project listings. Filter by layer and status to find what you need.
